mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, equity risk premiums expand as the real discount rate rises above historical norms. This compression of present values of future corporate earnings produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons month-over-month signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in > 40% of rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while housing starts (HOUST) simultaneously fall below 1,200 thousand units, aggregate demand weakens. This joint contraction signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines MoM AND HOUST < 1200 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistically significant incremental predictive power beyond individual series (p > 0.10 in Granger causality test).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines below 102.0 index points, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk. When combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 310), the mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as real earnings expectations compress.
regime_hint: stagflation regime with supply constraints and persistent inflation
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO × CPIAUCSL) shows no significant predictive power for 5-day returns (p > 0.15), or SPX produces positive returns in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.2% in a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold crossing produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.2% in any single month
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive returns in > 55% of months following UNRATE > 4.2%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter above 2.5% annualized signals strong economic expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as corporate earnings growth accelerates in line with GDP expansion.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation becomes negative in > 45% of rolling 8-quarter periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) falls below 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 305, monetary policy becomes accommodative relative to inflation. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rates turn negative and equity valuations expand.
failure_condition: The real rate proxy (FEDFUNDS - CPIAUCSL inflation rate) shows no significant predictive power for 60-day returns (p > 0.12), or SPX produces negative returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 102.5 index points while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 158,000 persons monthly. This joint supply-and-labor expansion signals productivity gains and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as unit labor cost expectations decline.
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (p > 0.14), or SPX produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
